summary: Archer Aviation's TSR from IPO (Sept 2021) to May 2026 is -33%, driven primarily by valuation multiple compression from $3.8B (SPAC) to $633M (Dec 2022 trough) to $4.9B (current), reflecting market skepticism of pre-revenue eVTOL profitability timelines. A secondary drag is 95% share dilution (390M to 760M shares) from $1.93B in capital raises, which destroyed per-share value despite stable enterprise value. Regulatory milestone achievement (4+ FAA certifications, USAF delivery) and strategic partnerships (United, Boeing, Stellantis) provided positive sentiment but were overwhelmed by repeated dilutive raises and sector-wide eVTOL downturn. Revenue inflection will be the next TSR catalyst.

rows.label: Valuation Multiple Compression / Re-rating
rows.value: -$3.2B (58%) from peak valuation to trough; currently -30% below IPO level

extras.correlation_to_tsr: Stock fell from $18.60 pre-IPO to $1.62 (Dec 2022) to $6.28 (May 2026). Market repriced from SPAC optimism to recognition that commercial timelines extend well beyond 2024. Valuation re-rating is the dominant TSR driver (-60% to -70% of return drag). Even strong FAA milestones did not prevent multiple compression due to pre-revenue risk and sector skepticism on unit economics.

rows.label: Share Dilution from Repeated Capital Raises
rows.value: +95% share count expansion ($1.93B raised over 8 rounds)

extras.correlation_to_tsr: Each capital raise coincided with stock weakness. Company maintained ~$3.7B-$4.9B market cap while share count doubled, destroying per-share ownership value by ~50%. Major rounds: $600M PIPE (2021), $175M (July 2023), $220M (Aug 2024, halved stock price that year), $300M (Feb 2025), $850M (June 2025, 14% drop). Dilution is second-order TSR driver (-30% to -40% of return drag).

rows.label: Regulatory Milestone Achievement (FAA Certifications & Flight Tests)
rows.value: 7 major milestones: Special Airworthiness (June 2023), Final Airworthiness Criteria (May 2024), Part 135 Carrier (June 2024), Transition Flight (June 2024), USAF Delivery (Aug 2024), Pilot Academy Cert (2025), 400+ test flights

extras.metric: FAA certification milestones and flight test achievement
extras.delta_metric: 4 FAA certificates obtained; Midnight transitioned at 100+ mph, 6,500 lbs payload
extras.correlation_to_tsr: Positive milestones but insufficient to drive positive TSR. August 2023 USAF announcement + $215M investment round sparked 40% rally, but was eroded by subsequent dilution. Regulatory progress prevents downside (bankruptcy risk) but market prices in success; does not reward execution until revenue appears. Milestones demonstrate capability but pre-revenue status limits TSR impact.

rows.label: Strategic Partnerships & Customer Commitments
rows.value: $1.5B+ in conditional commitments: United Airlines ($1B order + $10M prepayment), Stellantis (mfg + $400M commitment), Boeing (equity), USAF (first delivery)

extras.delta_metric: $1.5B+ conditional commitments vs. $0 revenue realized to date
extras.correlation_to_tsr: Partnerships are positive sentiment drivers but limited TSR impact because all commitments are pre-revenue and conditional. United $1B order and Boeing participation were catalyst for SPAC announcement (Feb 2021), but partnerships have not materialized into revenue. Stellantis manufacturing partnership ($400M labor cost funding) and USAF delivery (Aug 2024) endorse technology but do not drive TSR until revenue recognition begins. Partnership announcements often coincided with capital raises, neutralizing positive sentiment.

rows.label: Sector-Wide eVTOL Downturn & Peer Comparison
rows.value: Lilium bankruptcy (Feb 2025, ~$1.4B cumulative losses); Joby low of $3.31 (2023, ~$1B+ losses); Archer losses of $457M (2023), $1.14B cumulative (5-year)

extras.metric: Sector-wide eVTOL performance and peer losses
extras.delta_metric: All major players (Lilium, Joby, Archer) hit multi-year lows 2022-2023; Archer rebounded to $12.47 (Jan 2025) vs. peers
extras.correlation_to_tsr: Broad eVTOL sector skepticism 2022-2024 created macro headwind affecting all players equally. Archer outperformed peers in recovery, suggesting stronger relative execution (faster certifications, stronger partnerships), but still subject to sector-wide sentiment on timelines and unit economics. Sector downturn amplified valuation compression at Archer despite milestone achievement.
